# Constraints Requirements Checklist: UC-26 Create Custom Scheduling Constraints

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of the scheduling-constraints requirements before implementation and review
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/026-branch-scheduling-constraints/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the allowed scheduling-constraint types fully enumerated beyond the current examples, including whether additional constraint categories are intentionally out of scope? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK002 Are the term-context requirements defined for all constraint operations, including create, edit, delete, and later reuse during schedule generation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK003 Are the requirements explicit about whether constraint edits replace the full saved set or support partial per-entry persistence? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK004 Are the requirements defined for how an empty constraint set should be represented and treated after the last constraint is deleted? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "time-based availability rules" defined with enough precision to distinguish blocked times from start-time preferences and other temporal rules? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK006 Is "outside the allowed term context" clarified with specific disallowed conditions such as wrong term, unavailable instructor, or unsupported course reference? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK007 Is the threshold for "conflict" between constraints defined clearly enough to separate warning-only conflicts from invalid malformed input? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Clarifications]
- [x] CHK008 Is "understandable without technical interpretation" translated into objective message-quality criteria that reviewers can evaluate consistently? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-002]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Do the user stories, functional requirements, and edge cases use consistent terminology for "constraint set," "constraint entry," and "saved constraints"? [Consistency, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK010 Are the warning-confirmation requirements consistent between the clarification note, User Story 2, FR-006, and the conflicting-constraint success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK011 Are deletion requirements consistent between User Story 3, the edge-case section, and FR-010 about whether deleting the final entry leaves an empty valid set? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Are the success criteria measurable without depending on implementation-specific test setup details beyond the stated acceptance scenarios? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Can reviewers objectively determine what qualifies as "available to later schedule generation" from the written requirements alone? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK014 Is the save-failure requirement measurable enough to distinguish preserved prior state from partially overwritten state? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-002]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are alternate-flow requirements specified for editing an existing saved set that already contains previously confirmed conflicting constraints? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK016 Are recovery-flow requirements defined for what the student can do immediately after a save failure besides seeing the failure message? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK017 Are requirements specified for switching between registration terms while constraints exist for multiple terms? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-012]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are overlapping-time requirements explicit about whether the system consolidates entries, rejects them, or always routes them through warning confirmation? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK019 Are the requirements clear about what happens when referenced instructor or course data becomes unavailable after constraints were previously saved? [Edge Case, Dependency, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK020 Are concurrency-related requirements intentionally excluded or missing for cases where the same student's constraint set is edited in multiple sessions? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-014]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK021 Are accessibility requirements sufficiently specified beyond message readability, including keyboard access for warning confirmation and deletion flows? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-002, Spec §NFR-003]
- [x] CHK022 Are privacy and authorization requirements complete for stored professor preferences and term-scoped constraint data, beyond "only the authenticated student can view or modify" them? [Completeness, Spec §NFR-004]
- [x] CHK023 Are performance requirements defined for validation-heavy warning scenarios as clearly as for successful save scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-001, Spec §NFR-005, Spec §SC-005]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are the assumptions about available course and instructor data validated by explicit fallback requirements when those dependencies are stale, incomplete, or temporarily unavailable? [Assumption, Dependency, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK025 Does the spec clearly separate guaranteed requirements from planning assumptions about future schedule-generation behavior? [Consistency, Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Summary]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Review on 2026-03-11 tightened the spec around constraint-type scope, term isolation, full-set replacement semantics, empty-set handling, stale-data recovery, concurrent edit protection, accessibility, and measurable save outcomes.
